learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate leadership theories, styles and philosophies in a service and hospitality context.
Self-evaluate your leadership style to effectively utilise personal strengths and beliefs in leading people and service organisations.
Consider the importance of long term focus, and how to align hospitality organisational aims, mission, vision, values and strategies.
Analyse hospitality businesses in order to identify critical leadership success factors.

assessments.task_description:
Students will use the Leadership Compass and the Team Inventory Tools provided to evaluate their own leadership traits, skills and values in a 1000 word reflection.

First, use the Leadership Compass to evaluate your own leadership traits, skills and values. The result of the Leadership Compass will provide a dominant leadership perspective.

Second, complete the Team Inventory to determine your preferences in working with others and making decisions when working in teams.

Once these tools have provided you with results. You will write up a personal reflection that shares your results and an analysis of how, where and when these results have been present in your academic, professional or personal life.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via the Turnitin submission point on the course Learn.UQ site
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Weight: 40%

Due date: Friday 9 October 2026, 2:00pm

Submission: Electronically to Turnitin (via Blackboard)

Word limit: 2,500 words

This is an individual report in which you analyse and evaluate your own leadership practice in a service context. Rather than simply labelling yourself with one leadership ‘style’, you will examine how you actually lead, the various approaches you draw on, and evaluate your leadership across three connected dimensions: your own development as a leader, your impact on others and teams, and your ethical responsibilities. Throughout, situate your analysis in the service and hospitality context: your leadership shapes the employee experience, which in turn shapes the guest experience (e.g. the service-profit chain).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via the Turnitin submission point on the course Learn.UQ site
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is a problem-based assessment whereby students will have to apply service leadership principles, theories and approaches to address real-world situations and challenges facing all service industries - with a focus on the tourism, hospitality and events sectors. The assessment will assess your understanding of the content from all modules we have covered this semester. 

This is a 2 hour exam paper with 10 minutes reading time. The format of the assessment includes short answer questions in which you will be asked to apply concepts covered this semester to solve various service leadership problems and scenarios.

More details will be provided in class, as well as preparation advice.

Criteria & Marking:

Further information will be provided on Blackboard and in class.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Course Introduction / Linking Leadership to the Service and Hospitality Context

Leadership vs management; the hospitality leadership context; the experience economy; moments of truth and frontline discretion; the service-profit chain introduced as the course spine; bridging from service management to leadership.

Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Course Introduction / Linking Leadership to the Service and Hospitality Context
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Introduction / Linking Leadership to the Service and Hospitality Context Leadership vs management; the hospitality leadership context; the experience economy; moments of truth and frontline discretion; the service-profit chain introduced as the course spine; bridging from service management to leadership.

learning_activities.topic:
Leadership Identity and Self-Awareness

Leadership identity and self-concept; personal values and values clarification; strengths-based leadership; self-awareness and reflective practice; the Leadership Compass as a diagnostic of your dominant perspective.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership Identity and Self-Awareness Leadership identity and self-concept; personal values and values clarification; strengths-based leadership; self-awareness and reflective practice; the Leadership Compass as a diagnostic of your dominant perspective.

learning_activities.topic:
Leading with Others: Team Roles and Followership

Team dynamics and team roles; followership and why people choose to follow; collaboration, conflict styles and working across differences; the Team Inventory as a diagnostic; interdependence in frontline service teams.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Leading with Others: Team Roles and Followership
learning_activities.topic_description: Leading with Others: Team Roles and Followership Team dynamics and team roles; followership and why people choose to follow; collaboration, conflict styles and working across differences; the Team Inventory as a diagnostic; interdependence in frontline service teams.

learning_activities.topic:
Trait, Behavioural and Skills Approaches

Trait, behavioural and skills approaches to leadership; technical, human and conceptual skills; strengths and limitations of leader-centred theories; applying these lenses to interpret your Compass and Team Inventory results.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Trait, Behavioural and Skills Approaches Trait, behavioural and skills approaches to leadership; technical, human and conceptual skills; strengths and limitations of leader-centred theories; applying these lenses to interpret your Compass and Team Inventory results.

learning_activities.topic:
Situational and Adaptive Leadership

Contingency thinking and matching style to context; situational leadership and follower readiness; adaptive leadership (technical vs adaptive challenges); leadership flexibility; flexing to guests, staff and shifting demand.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Situational and Adaptive Leadership Contingency thinking and matching style to context; situational leadership and follower readiness; adaptive leadership (technical vs adaptive challenges); leadership flexibility; flexing to guests, staff and shifting demand.

learning_activities.topic:
Transformational and Authentic Leadership

Transformational leadership and the four I's; vision, empowerment and motivation; authentic leadership and trust; transactional vs transformational; inspiring discretionary effort in a guest-facing culture.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Transformational and Authentic Leadership Transformational leadership and the four I's; vision, empowerment and motivation; authentic leadership and trust; transactional vs transformational; inspiring discretionary effort in a guest-facing culture.

learning_activities.topic:
Emotional Intelligence and Leadership

Emotional intelligence and Goleman's dimensions; emotional labour (surface vs deep acting); empathy and reading the guest; communication, feedback and difficult conversations; the satisfaction mirror; technostress and AI on the frontline.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Emotional Intelligence and Leadership Emotional intelligence and Goleman's dimensions; emotional labour (surface vs deep acting); empathy and reading the guest; communication, feedback and difficult conversations; the satisfaction mirror; technostress and AI on the frontline.

learning_activities.topic:
Building and Leading Service Culture with a Service-Profit Chain Lens

Organisational culture; trust, psychological safety, employee engagement and discretionary effort; servant leadership and internal service quality — culture as the engine of the chain; leadership ethics in service — the leader's responsibilities to employees and guests, fairness and respect (introducing the ethical lens assessed in Assessment 2).

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Building and Leading Service Culture with a Service-Profit Chain Lens
learning_activities.topic_description: Building and Leading Service Culture with a Service-Profit Chain Lens Organisational culture; trust, psychological safety, employee engagement and discretionary effort; servant leadership and internal service quality — culture as the engine of the chain; leadership ethics in service — the leader's responsibilities to employees and guests, fairness and respect (introducing the ethical lens assessed in Assessment 2).

learning_activities.topic:
Strategic Leadership

Strategic thinking and vision; organisational purpose, mission, vision and values; aligning people, performance and purpose with a long-term focus; stakeholder management and responsibilities, and the consequences of strategic decisions (extending the ethical lens for Assessment 2).

Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Strategic Leadership Strategic thinking and vision; organisational purpose, mission, vision and values; aligning people, performance and purpose with a long-term focus; stakeholder management and responsibilities, and the consequences of strategic decisions (extending the ethical lens for Assessment 2).

learning_activities.topic:
Assessment 2 Workshop

Intensive workshopping of Assessment 2; interpreting your leadership practice through the literature and a personal experience; coaching on the relational and ethical evaluation criteria and your development plan; peer feedback and collaborative support.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Assessment 2 Workshop Intensive workshopping of Assessment 2; interpreting your leadership practice through the literature and a personal experience; coaching on the relational and ethical evaluation criteria and your development plan; peer feedback and collaborative support.

learning_activities.topic:
Leading Change and Innovation

Change leadership and resistance to change; innovation and experimentation; digital transformation and AI in hospitality; the reimagined service-profit chain (support-AI and experience-AI); leading people through disruption.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Leading Change and Innovation Change leadership and resistance to change; innovation and experimentation; digital transformation and AI in hospitality; the reimagined service-profit chain (support-AI and experience-AI); leading people through disruption.

learning_activities.topic:
Leadership in Crisis and Uncertainty

Crisis leadership and leading under pressure; resilience and trust under pressure; decision-making and sensemaking in uncertainty; communication during crisis; the "repair" dimension of service recovery; hospitality's exposure to external shocks.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership in Crisis and Uncertainty Crisis leadership and leading under pressure; resilience and trust under pressure; decision-making and sensemaking in uncertainty; communication during crisis; the "repair" dimension of service recovery; hospitality's exposure to external shocks.

learning_activities.topic:
The Future Leader

The future of work and changing workforce expectations; sustainability and responsible leadership; leadership capabilities for the next decade; synthesis — revisiting the service-profit chain end-to-end and rewriting your leadership definition; does "unreasonable hospitality" scale? / Exam review and Course final remarks

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: The Future Leader The future of work and changing workforce expectations; sustainability and responsible leadership; leadership capabilities for the next decade; synthesis — revisiting the service-profit chain end-to-end and rewriting your leadership definition; does "unreasonable hospitality" scale? /  Exam review and Course final remarks
